Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additive manufacturing of an ultrastrong, deformable Al alloy with nanoscale intermetallics

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition Al92Ti2Fe2Co2Ni2 (at%); powder size -53+15 μm gas atomized by Atlantic Equipment Engineering, Inc.

Process_Text.original: LPBF/SLM fabrication using SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer with 400 W IPG fiber laser (λ=1070 nm); laser power 200 W; scan speed 1200 mm/s; hatch space 100 μm; layer thickness 30 μm; laser spot 70 μm diameter; build plate preheated to 200°C; layer rotation 67°; Argon atmosphere with oxygen level below 1000 PPM
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF at 200 W laser power, 1200 mm/s scan speed, 100 μm hatch, 30 μm layer thickness, 200°C build plate preheat, 67° layer rotation, Ar atmosphere

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer; IPG fiber laser (400 W, λ=1070 nm)

Microstructure_Text.original: Heterogeneous microstructure with melt pools ~120 μm width and ~80 μm depth; Coarse rosettes in melt pool center containing Al3Ti cores with alternating Al3Ti and Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 lamellae (150-300 nm thickness); 36 vol.% coarse rosettes, 4 vol.% cellular precipitates (Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2), 60 vol.% Al matrix; Ample defects in Al3Ti phase; dislocation density 4.7×10^13 to 1.0×10^14 m^-2
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Melt pool structure with coarse rosettes (36 vol.%), cellular Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 precipitates (4 vol.%), and Al matrix (60 vol.%)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; bulk compression test on 6×12 mm cylinder; strain rate 10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: MTS framework with 30 kN load cell; cylindrical sample 6 mm diameter × 12 mm height
Properties_Info.Note: Engineering stress exceeding 800 MPa with substantial plastic deformability around 20%; barreling phenomenon observed
Substantial plastic deformability; barreling phenomenon typical of ductile metallic materials

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Considère criterion analysis; true stress-strain curves with work hardening
Properties_Info.Note: 7% uniform compression strain in early stages followed by long toughing stages

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition Al92Ti2Fe2Co2Ni2 (at%); powder size -53+15 μm gas atomized by Atlantic Equipment Engineering, Inc.

Process_Text.original: LPBF/SLM fabrication using SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer with 400 W IPG fiber laser (λ=1070 nm); laser power 250 W; scan speed 1200 mm/s; hatch space 100 μm; layer thickness 30 μm; laser spot 70 μm diameter; build plate preheated to 200°C; layer rotation 67°; Argon atmosphere with oxygen level below 1000 PPM
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF at 250 W laser power, 1200 mm/s scan speed, 100 μm hatch, 30 μm layer thickness, 200°C build plate preheat, 67° layer rotation, Ar atmosphere

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer; IPG fiber laser (400 W, λ=1070 nm)

Microstructure_Text.original: Heterogeneous microstructure with melt pools ~120 μm width and ~80 μm depth; Coarse rosettes in melt pool center; fine rosettes near melt pool boundaries; similar phase constitution to 200 W; finer microstructure expected with higher laser energy density
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Melt pool structure with heterogeneous rosette distribution; coarse rosettes dominant in center, fine rosettes near boundaries

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; bulk compression test on 6×12 mm cylinder; strain rate 10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: MTS framework with 30 kN load cell; cylindrical sample 6 mm diameter × 12 mm height
Properties_Info.Note: Flow stress decreases to 550 MPa at higher laser energy

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition Al92Ti2Fe2Co2Ni2 (at%); powder size -53+15 μm gas atomized by Atlantic Equipment Engineering, Inc.

Process_Text.original: LPBF/SLM fabrication using SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer with 400 W IPG fiber laser (λ=1070 nm); laser power 300 W; scan speed 1200 mm/s; hatch space 100 μm; layer thickness 30 μm; laser spot 70 μm diameter; build plate preheated to 200°C; layer rotation 67°; Argon atmosphere with oxygen level below 1000 PPM; Used for micropillar compression and detailed microstructural characterization
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF at 300 W laser power, 1200 mm/s scan speed, 100 μm hatch, 30 μm layer thickness, 200°C build plate preheat, 67° layer rotation, Ar atmosphere

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125 HL metal 3D printer; IPG fiber laser (400 W, λ=1070 nm)

Microstructure_Text.original: Melt pools ~120 μm width and ~80 μm depth with layer rotations; Heterogeneous microstructure: coarse rosettes (36 vol.%) in melt pool center with lamellae 150-300 nm; fine rosettes (97 vol.%) near boundaries with lamellae 20-60 nm; cellular Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 precipitates (4 vol.%) in coarse region; Al matrix (60 vol.% in center, 3 vol.% near boundaries); Al3Ti cores with alternating Al3Ti and Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 lamellae; orientation relationships: [130]Al3Ti // [100]Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2, (002)Al3Ti // (001)Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2, (310)Al3Ti // (010)Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2; lattice mismatch 2.5% and 2.1%
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Melt pool structure with coarse rosettes (36 vol.%) in center, fine rosettes (97 vol.%) near boundaries; Al3Ti and Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 intermetallics with nanoscale lamellae

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; nanoindentation with Berkovich tip
Properties_Info.Note: Higher Young's modulus (140-150 GPa) at melt pool boundaries; lower (130-140 GPa) in interior

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; nanoindentation with Berkovich tip; displacement-control mode at 800 nm depth; 100×100 μm area with 121 indents, 10 μm spacing
Properties_Info.Note: Hardness varies across melt pools: 3.5-4.5 GPa at boundaries (finer microstructure), 2.5-3.0 GPa in interior

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; bulk compression test on 6×12 mm cylinder; strain rate 10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: MTS framework with 30 kN load cell; cylindrical sample 6 mm diameter × 12 mm height
Properties_Info.Note: Flow stress 550 MPa with 5-20% compressive strain at 300 W

Composition_Info.Note: Micropillar compression on fine rosette region (near melt pool boundaries)

Process_Text.original: LPBF/SLM with 300 W laser power; Fine rosette region near melt pool boundaries with 97 vol.% fine rosettes, 3 vol.% Al matrix; Lamellae thickness 20-60 nm
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF 300 W; Fine rosette region with nanoscale intermetallics (20-60 nm lamellae)

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125 HL; Hysitron PI 88×R PicoIndenter for micropillar compression

Microstructure_Text.original: Fine rosette region near melt pool boundaries with densely packed fine rosettes (97 vol.%); Al3Ti cores with alternating 20-60 nm lamellae of Al3Ti and Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2; limited Al matrix content (3 vol.%); Uniform co-deformation of matrix and precipitates
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine rosettes (97 vol.%) with 20-60 nm lamellae; Al3Ti cores with Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 alternating layers

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; micropillar compression with loading-unloading hysteresis loops; back stress from σb = (σ1+σ2)/2
Properties_Info.Note: Very high back stress in fine rosette region; σ1=unloading yield stress, σ2=loading yield stress

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; in situ micropillar compression in SEM; 10 μm height, 5 μm diameter pillar; strain rate 5×10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Micropillar: 10 μm height × 5 μm diameter, FIB fabricated; 10 and 20 μm diamond flat-punch tips used
Properties_Info.Note: Fine rosette region can exceed 900 MPa; true flow stress reaches 1 GPa; Engineering stress 1.18 GPa; At least 15% plasticity

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; in situ SEM micropillar compression
Properties_Info.Note: At least 15% plasticity in fine rosette region; retention of cylindrical shape indicates uniform co-deformation

Composition_Info.Note: Micropillar compression on coarse rosette region (melt pool center)

Process_Text.original: LPBF/SLM with 300 W laser power; Coarse rosette region in melt pool center with 36 vol.% coarse rosettes, 4 vol.% cellular Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 precipitates, 60 vol.% Al matrix; Lamellae thickness 150-300 nm
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF 300 W; Coarse rosette region with larger intermetallics (150-300 nm lamellae)

Process_Info.Equipment: SLM 125 HL; Hysitron PI 88×R PicoIndenter for micropillar compression

Microstructure_Text.original: Coarse rosette region in melt pool center with 36 vol.% coarse rosettes; 4 vol.% cellular Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 precipitates (yellow arrows); 60 vol.% Al matrix; Al3Ti cores with 150-300 nm alternating lamellae; Rosette precipitates readily extrude to outer surfaces during compression; Preferential plastic flow in soft Al matrix; Insignificant plasticity in hard intermetallics precipitates
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarse rosettes (36 vol.%) with 150-300 nm lamellae; cellular Al9(Fe,Co,Ni)2 (4 vol.%); Al matrix (60 vol.%)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; micropillar compression with loading-unloading hysteresis loops; back stress from σb = (σ1+σ2)/2
Properties_Info.Note: Lower back stress (~250 MPa) in coarse rosette region compared to fine rosette region (~600 MPa)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K; in situ micropillar compression in SEM; 10 μm height, 5 μm diameter pillar; strain rate 5×10^-3 s^-1
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Micropillar: 10 μm height × 5 μm diameter, FIB fabricated; 10 and 20 μm diamond flat-punch tips used
Properties_Info.Note: Coarse rosette region has lower flow stress of 500 MPa compared to fine rosette region
